task.domains: (topic task) (topic hi-d) (topic ui-d) (topic sw-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic ut-d) a(base ishcondition) a(base varid) a(base varref) a(base cid) a(base partslist) (topic oce-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceMetaDecl-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c task oceTask-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c)
task.title: How to install-reinstall-upgrade-downgrade the system software

abstract.p: This document provides information about the different ways to install, reinstall, upgrade and downgrade the system software and links to the related service procedure to be used in each case.

section.title: 1. What is a software installation
section.p: A software installation can be performed on an empty hard disk drive (in case a faulty disk was replaced) or a non-empty disk (the software become unstable and has to be reinstalled).
The installation is made with a USB key.
This installation procedure can only be used by Service.

p.b: a. Installation on an empty disk

section.p: The software is installed from scratch.
At the installation USB key creation time, “Keeping the existing jobs and settings” can be selected.
This setting is ignored in case of installation on an empty disk:

li.p: Service, engine and user configurations are lost (after the installation completed, a backup (if available) has to be manually restored)
All job files and accounting data which were present on the printer when the previous disk failed are lost

p.b: b. Installation on a non-empty disk

section.p: At the installation USB key creation time, ‘Keeping the existing jobs and settings’ can be selected.
This setting is taken into account in case of installation on a non-empty disk:

li.p: Service, engine and user configurations are preserved (no restore needed)
All job files present on the printer are kept
If still possible, they can be saved with Settings Editor (Configuration > Accounting > Save account log files) but they cannot be restored on the printer after the installation completed.

section.title: 2. What is a software upgrade/downgrade
section.p: A software upgrade/downgrade is performed with Settings Editor.
A valid software is present on the printer hard disk drive.

section.title: 3. How to install the printer software

li.p: To install the printer software on an empty hard disk drive:
See 11 How to do a system software installation on an empty disk.
To reinstall the printer software on a non-empty hard disk drive:
See 11 How to do a system software installation on a non-empty disk.

section.title: How to upgrade the printer software

li.p: See 11 How to upgrade the system software with Settings Editor
